title: Auth Service Degraded — EU-West Session Cache Exhaustion
rca: The refresh token rotation feature deployed at 23:45 UTC introduced a 24-hour TTL for token pairs, causing Redis in eu-west-1 to store both old and new tokens during the rotation window. Over 12 hours of EU-West user activity, the cache grew to 92% memory utilization, triggering aggressive LRU eviction of active sessions. This caused ~30% of EU-West auth token validations to miss cache and fall back to the database, producing 4-6 second latency spikes and session invalidations.
resolution_steps: 1. Initiated Redis key flush for expired rotation tokens at 11:23 UTC
2. Redis memory reduced to 74% — latency recovering
3. Hotfix PR: reduce rotation TTL from 24h to 1h — in review
business_impact: Approximately 30% of EU-West region users experiencing session drops and authentication failures. 3 enterprise accounts impacted including Salesforce integration disruption. No US-region impact. Estimated 2,400 affected sessions.
workaround: Manual Redis flush in progress — partially restoring service. Full fix requires hotfix deployment.

communication_log.note: Elevated auth latency detected by SRE monitoring
Root cause identified — Redis cache exhaustion from token rotation TTL bug
